cases.note: percent-encodes the :: host separator so it survives pane-key ':' delimiting
also encodes slashes and spaces in the host surface id
round-trips a wrapped id back to the original host surface id
host separator without the web-terminal prefix is not decoded
prefixed but malformed percent-escape: TS catch returns the full original tabId, the Rust port returns the decoded slice
cases.allowDivergence: On a malformed decode, TS returns the full original tabId ('web-terminal-%zz') while orca_core::terminal_surface_id::to_host_session_tab_id returns the encoded slice ('%zz').

cases.note: prefixed id is a web-terminal surface tab id
host separator without the prefix is not a web-terminal surface tab id
